title: Yon-Rogg
content:
{{CharHub|Yon-Rogg|Yon-Rogg}} {{Character |image = Yon-Rogg Textless Poster.jpg |real name = Yon-Rogg |species = [[Kree]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|KRE}} |gender = Male |title = Commander |affiliation = {{Affiliation|STAR}} |actor = [[Jude Law]] |movie = {{Infobox List|''[[Captain Marvel (film)|Captain Marvel]]''
{{Collapse|''[[The Marvels]]'' (flashbacks)}}}} |book = ''[[Captain Marvel: Starforce on the Rise]]''
''[[Captain Marvel: StarForce Mission Log]]''
''[[Captain Marvel (audiobook)|Captain Marvel]]'' |status = Alive}} {{Quote|I'm so proud of you. You've come a long way since that [[Assassination of Mar-Vell|day]] I found you by the lake. But can you keep your emotions in check long enough to take me on? Or will they get the better of you as always? I always told you, you'll be ready the day you'll knock me down as yourself. This is that moment. This is that moment, Vers!|Yon-Rogg to [[Captain Marvel]]|Captain Marvel (film)}} '''Yon-Rogg''' is a [[Kree Empire]] commander and leader of [[Starforce]], with a devout loyalty to the [[Supreme Intelligence]] and the Kree ideologies. During the [[Kree-Skrull War]], Yon-Rogg and [[Minn-Erva]] were dispatched to [[Assassination of Mar-Vell|kill]] [[Mar-Vell]] on [[Planet C-53]], seeking a [[Tesseract]]-[[Light-Speed Engine|powered energy core]] that she was developing. Yon-Rogg later took under his tutelage [[Vers]], a [[United States Air Force]] pilot who absorbed the core's energy during its destruction. Years later, at the prospect of Kree victory, the Starforce had cornered the [[Skrull]] population, including [[Talos]], to C-53. When Vers learned of her true origins on C-53, Yon-Rogg was authorized by [[Ronan the Accuser]] to capture her. Yon-Rogg failed to wipe out the Skrull refugees and steal back the Tesseract and was defeated by Vers, who sent him back to [[Hala]] with a warning to the Supreme Intelligence. ==Biography== ===[[Kree-Skrull War]]=== ====[[Assassination of Mar-Vell]]==== {{Quote|The energy core, where is it?"
"Pararescue's on the way. You have two minutes until you're surrounded."
"Then I see no reason to prolong this conversation.|Yon-Rogg and [[Carol Danvers]]|Captain Marvel (film)}} [[File:Yon-Rogg 1989.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg looks at aftermath of the crash]] Yon-Rogg was sent along with [[Minn-Erva]] by the [[Supreme Intelligence]] to retrieve the energy core of the [[Light-Speed Engine]] created by [[Mar-Vell]] after the latter betrayed her race. He intercepted her and [[Carol Danvers]] during a flight and shot them out of the sky. After the plane crash-landed, Yon-Rogg shot Mar-Vell with a [[Kree Pistol]], killing her. [[File:Yon-Rogg Dives.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg avoids a massive blast of energy]] He then demanded from Danvers the location of the engine's energy core, with the latter shooting it and creating a huge explosion, resulting in her absorption of the energy. Yon-Rogg was then informed by Minn-Erva that the core was completely destroyed and that Danvers was still moving. He decided to take Danvers to the Kree, believing they could turn her into a weapon.{{Ref|CM}} Although Danvers did not survive the explosion, Yon-Rogg resurrected her with a blood transfusion and wiped her memories.''[[Captain Marvel: The Official Movie Special]]'' ====Aphos Prime Experiment==== {{Quote|What happened on [[Aphos Prime]] is a mystery to us all.|Yon-Rogg to [[Vers]]|Captain Marvel: Starforce on the Rise}} Yon-Rogg briefed [[Vers]] and [[Minn-Erva]] on a routine reconnaissance mission to [[Aphos Prime]], when Vers started asking questions. He explained that a scout ship had been destroyed without survivors after reporting that [[Skrulls]] were on the planet. He told the two soldiers to determine the Skrull force's strength and report back without engaging the Skrulls, but Vers suggested that they would engage first. Vers asked why they would send [[Starforce]] members on a reconnaissance mission, prompting an argument between her and Minn-Erva about following orders which Yon-Rogg interrupted by explaining that the orders came from the [[Supreme Intelligence]]. Vers said that she understood, but Yon-Rogg knew that she did not mean it. After briefing, Yon-Rogg approached Vers and told her that their place was to follow the [[Supreme Intelligence]]'s orders without question for the good of the [[Kree Empire]]. He told Vers that their mission was more important than she knew, prompting Vers to ask why. She suggested they should stop the Skrulls, so Yon-Rogg assured her that they would eventually. He told Vers to consider it a test, prompting her to realize that his intention was to test her and Minn-Erva, which Yon-Rogg would not confirm. Vers and Minn-Erva returned from their mission with a [[Skrull]] prisoner. Yon-Rogg told Vers that he would be interrogated as they prepared for a briefing. Vers asked if Yon-Rogg knew about how dangerous Aphos Prime was, which he denied. However, Yon-Rogg then secretly made a note on his tablet at the test involving Aphos Prime and [[Peer Kaal]] was complete.''[[Captain Marvel: Starforce on the Rise]]'' ====Sy'gyl Mission==== {{Quote|I know you are good enough to be a part of this team. But you need to know it.|Yon-Rogg to [[Vers]]|Captain Marvel: Starforce on the Rise}} One morning, Yon-Rogg attended a short briefing in which he received a mission which was concisely communicated to him, although he found it difficult to swallow. He assured himself that the rest of [[Starforce]] would be ready for the mission, but realized that the mission would require cooperation and that he would therefore need to watch [[Vers]]. Later that day, he was met by Vers, and the two started running together. They went to the gym while Vers told him about a man she had run into while jogging, causing Yon-Rogg to realize she was unfocused. They arrived at the gym and sparred together, during which Vers joked. Yon-Rogg realized that Vers was taking her training too lightly and needed to learn never to drop her guard. Yon-Rogg defeated her and told her that she would be killed if she did not take it seriously, but Vers insisted that she was before throwing a towel in his face and kicking his neck. Yon-Rogg later arrived at the briefing room with Starforce and explained the [[Kree]]'s [[Kree-Skrull War|war]] against the [[Skrulls]] was ongoing and required an increasing amount of resources. He explained that this has led the Kree to consider ending their [[Kree-Nova War|war]] against [[Xandar]], upsetting [[Minn-Erva]] and [[Att-Lass]]. Yon-Rogg explained that the team was tasked with infiltrating the Xandarian-controlled planet [[Sy'gyl]] and receiving plans from a [[Xandarian]] defector for a weapon. Vers asked why the Kree would risk peace for the mission, so [[Korath]] and Yon-Rogg explained that the Kree wanted the upper hand during the peace, and that the weapon plans would give them that. Yon-Rogg then ordered the team to prepare and meet at the hangar in less than an hour. At the hangar, Vers saw that they were not flying the ''[[Helion]]'', so Yon-Rogg explained that they could not afford to be seen. While flying to Sy'gyl, the team was attacked by Kree fighters mistaking them as Xandarian. Vers suggested that they fire back, causing the entire team to look at her. However, Yon-Rogg agreed it would help avoid suspicion, so he passed the order to their pilot, [[Sun-Val]]. During the ensuing battle, a hole formed in the hull, causing air to leave the ship. However, Vers managed to activate the shield and stop the ship from losing too much air. Yon-Rogg complimented Vers for saving them. As they entered the planet's upper atmosphere, Sun-Val commented on the quality of the ship. Yon-Rogg made fun of Korath for picking it, but Korath got defensive. Yon-Rogg therefore asked Vers to help explain that he was making fun of Korath. As the ship entered cruising speed, Starforce started preparing, and Yon-Rogg called Vers over to talk to her. He and Korath told Vers that she would be infiltrating the base on Sy'gyl by herself, confusing her. Korath scolded Vers for questioning orders, so Yon-Rogg told him to check the weapons with the others. Yon-Rogg then told Vers that the decision came from the [[Supreme Intelligence]], explaining that she would be able to succeed. As the ship landed, Yon-Rogg opened the hatch and told Starforce that Minn-Erva would be the only one with a gun. They started traversing the planet when Vers joked to herself and Minn-Erva asked what she said. Yon-Rogg told them to be quiet and communicate with hand signals. When Bron-Char saw something that gave the team pause, Yon-Rogg sent Att-Lass to investigate, and he came back reporting that it was a Xandarian ship. Yon-Rogg continued having Att-Lass scout ahead while the rest of Starforce trailed behind, and made sure Minn-Erva could see the team. He showed Vers a diagram of the bunker she would be infiltrating, which Vers found to be easy. However, Yon-Rogg insisted that they assume it would be difficult. Korath accused Vers of not taking the mission seriously, but Yon-Rogg insisted that she was. She told Vers to find the plans on the third level and to look for a scientist named [[Peer Kaal]], showing her a holographic image of her. Yon-Rogg then watched as Vers left, assuring himself that she could succeed. Yon-Rogg checked on Minn-Erva, who reported that Vers had successfully entered the bunker. Yon-Rogg noted that Vers might not have a signal, when Minn-Erva suddenly saw multiple [[Nova Corps]] men approaching. Yon-Rogg took [[Bron-Char]] with him to deal with them, ordering Korath and Att-Lass to stay behind in case others arrived. The two returned to the freighter and found [[Sun-Val/Skrull|Sun-Val]] on the way. They came across Vers and [[Rhomann Dey]], and Yon-Rogg commented on how loud the two were talking. Vers joined with Yon-Rogg who dismissed and thanked Dey. The three returned to the freighter, where Minn-Erva shouted for Vers to get down as Sun-Val prepared to kill Vers. Yon-Rogg and Bron-Char shouted the same warning as Minn-Erva killed Sun-Val, who revealed herself to be a Skrull who had killed the real Sun-Val. Yon-Rogg realized that the Skrull wanted to steal the plans from Vers, who reported that she had in fact retrieved them. On the flight back, Yon-Rogg checked on Vers, telling her to go to the med-bay when she returned. Vers asked about something Dey had said to her, although Yon-Rogg told her that Xandarians were not to be trusted and asked if it mattered what he said before walking away. ====Training with Vers==== [[File:Captainmarvel-movie-screencaps com-175.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg getting woken up to train by [[Vers]]]] {{Quote|I slipped."
"Right, you slipped as a result of me punching you in the face."
"I was already slipping when you happened to punch me in the face.|[[Vers]] and Yon-Rogg|Captain Marvel (film)}} One morning, Yon-Rogg was woken up early by [[Vers]] because she couldn't sleep. He recommended that she take pills, but she denied it saying that she couldn't sleep because of dreams she's been having. She then invited Yon-Rogg to practice fighting with her, to which he accepted and followed her to another building. [[File:Marvell & Danver's Empire.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg trains [[Vers]] how to fight]] As soon as they started training, Yon-Rogg knocked Vers down by punching her in the face. She tried to cover her mistake by telling him that she slipped. As they continued fighting, Yon-Rogg asked Vers about her dreams, to which she replied that it wasn't anything new. He insisted that she forgets the past so that she could control her powers. He warned her about the dangers that emotions could bring if she let them loose. Vers accidentally throws Yon-Rogg in the air with a photon blast, prompting the latter to arrange a meeting with the [[Supreme Intelligence]] for Vers. [[File:Captain Marvel (film) 102.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg advises [[Vers]] to rely on her brain]] The pair then took a train to the Supreme Intelligence. On the way, Yon-Rogg told Vers that the Supreme Intelligence would take the form of the person that she admired the most. She asked him who he saw, only for him to refuse to give an answer saying that it is a sacred thing that no [[Kree]] divulged. Yon-Rogg advised Vers to rely less on her emotions and more on her brain so that her powers would not get out of control.{{Ref|CM}} ====[[Ambush in Torfa]]==== [[File:StarforceTVSpot.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg prepares his [[Starforce|team]] for the mission]] {{Quote|The [[Accusers]] will bomb a [[Skrull]] stronghold here in the south, we slip in, we locate [[Soh-Larr]], and we get out, leaving them without noise.|Yon-Rogg to [[Starforce]]|Captain Marvel (film)}} Yon-Rogg later received a mission for the [[Starforce]] from the [[Supreme Intelligence]]. He explained to his teammates that the [[Skrulls]] have invaded one of the planets at the border of the [[Kree Empire]], [[Torfa]] and that one of the [[Kree]] spies, [[Soh-Larr]] was compromised. He told them that the plan was for the [[Accusers]] to bomb a Skrull stronghold while the Starforce would silently rescue Soh-Larr. [[File:Captain Marvel (film) 142.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|The [[Starforce]] are attacked by [[Skrulls]]]] The team traveled to Torfa where they landed underwater and used their [[Starforce Uniform|suits]] to go to the surface. As they planned their attack, Vers suggested going alone, with Yon-Rogg declining the offer. As Starforce met some locals, Yon-Rogg tried to make them stand down. To everyone's shock, the locals were, in fact, [[Skrulls]], who started attacking the team. Yon-Rogg warned his teammates of the ambush and started fighting back. After Vers was captured by Talos disguised as Soh-Larr, Yon-Rogg and the Starforce retreated to the ''[[Helion]]'' and fled Torfa.{{Ref|CM}} ====Contacted by Vers==== [[File:YonRogg-DontForgetWhoYouAre.PNG|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg getting contacted by [[Vers]]]] {{Quote|I thought we lost you. Did you find [[Soh-Larr]]?"
"It wasn't Soh-Larr. [[Talos]] simmed him, even knew his code."
"That's impossible. That code was buried in his unconscious.|Yon-Rogg and [[Vers]]|Captain Marvel (film)}} After [[Vers]] escaped from [[Skrull]] captivity and crash-landed on Earth, she modified a payphone and contacted Yon-Rogg and the rest of [[Starforce]]. She used the Kree code system to identify herself and explained that "Soh-Larr" was actually [[Talos]] who even knew the Kree spy's identification code. Yon-Rogg noted that it was impossible as the code was buried in his unconscious, but Vers explained the memory probe the Skrulls used to search her memories. Vers told Yon-Rogg that she was on [[Planet C-53]] and that the Skrulls were looking for [[Wendy Lawson]] and the [[Light-Speed Engine]], but Yon-Rogg told her not to pursue them as she had been caught once already. Yon-Rogg then asked [[Att-Lass]] how far they were to C-53, to which he replied that they had 22 hours to the closest jump point. After their contact with Vers abruptly ended, [[Korath]] suspected that if the Skrulls had captured Vers she was compromised, but Yon-Rogg replied that she's stronger than he thinks.{{Ref|CM}} ====Learning of Wendy Lawson==== Yon-Rogg was suddenly contacted by [[Vers]] again. Vers told him that a [[Kree]] woman named [[Wendy Lawson]] was on [[Planet C-53]] and died in a plane crash. Yon-Rogg told her that he just discovered a mission report of that crash and there was only so much he could tell her, but Lawson was an undercover operative named Mar-Vell, who was working on an energy core that could help them win the war. Vers then told him that she saw pictures of herself and that she had a life on C-53. Yon-Rogg insisted that it could be a [[Skrull]] simulation. He then reminded her to not let her emotions overcloud her judgement.{{Ref|CM}} ====Contacted by Ronan==== [[File:Korath, Yon-Rogg & Ronan.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg assures [[Ronan]] of their mission]] Following the [[Ambush in Torfa]] and [[Vers]]' abduction, [[Ronan the Accuser|Ronan]] contacted Yon-Rogg and told him the [[Accusers]] completed their mission, but blamed the [[Starforce]] for the failure of theirs. Yon-Rogg told him it was a trap. Ronan told Yon-Rogg to give him Vers' location and the accusers would handle it, but Yon-Rogg insisted that the Starforce would handle it. Ronan reminded him that the [[Skrulls]] that got away are a threat. Yon-Rogg lied and told him that they are lost, but will be found. Before ending the call, Ronan told Yon-Rogg to find them or he will.{{Ref|CM}} ====Arriving on Earth==== {{Quote|What have you done to [[Captain Marvel|her]]?"
"You're too late.|Yon-Rogg and [[Norex]]|Captain Marvel (film)}} Yon-Rogg tracked [[Vers]]' beacon on [[Earth]] to a shack, where she tried to use the [[Kree]]'s code system to identify herself to him. He stopped her, noting that the code system was compromised. They then began asking each other questions that they thought a [[Skrull]] wouldn't be able to answer. When Yon-Rogg asked her about her earliest memory of [[Hala]], she correctly answered that it was a transfusion and blue blood running through her veins, but when he asked her whose blood it was, she was surprised, unable to answer. Yon-Rogg correctly deduced that it was a [[Norex|Skrull]] and shot him in the chest with his pistol, fatally wounding him. [[File:Ronan the Accuser & Yon-Rogg.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg calling for [[Ronan]]'s support]] In his final moments, Norex transformed back to his own Skrull shape. Yon-Rogg remarked to him that the blood coursing in Vers' veins was his, and demanded to know what the Skrulls had done to her. Norex replied by saying that Yon-Rogg was too late, before an angered Yon-Rogg executed him with a final shot. Seeing as Vers was compromised and knew everything, Yon-Rogg contacted [[Ronan the Accuser]], asking for his help to eradicate Skrulls from Earth. He then tracked Vers to [[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]].{{Ref|CM}} ====[[Battle at Mar-Vell's Laboratory]]==== [[File:Captain Marvel (film) 175.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg witnesses [[Vers]]' cosmic powers]] {{Quote|Arm wrestle for the [[Tesseract]]?"
"I used to find you amusing. Let's put an end to this.|[[Captain Marvel]] and Yon-Rogg|Captain Marvel (film)}} Yon-Rogg and the rest of the [[Starforce]] arrived at [[Mar-Vell]]'s orbital laboratory over [[Earth]] to capture [[Vers]], [[Skrulls]] and their allies. Seeing Vers' [[Starforce Uniform|suit]] with a different color scheme, Yon-Rogg asked what she had done to her uniform. When she said that he lied to her, he claimed that he turned her into the best version of herself. Yon-Rogg then remotely used her [[Photon Inhibitor|photon inhibitor]] to suppress her powers, and used his [[Magnitron Gauntlets]] to knock her out. He sent her to commune with the [[Supreme Intelligence]], but she was able to overpower it in a mental battle and destroy the Inhibitor, unlocking her full powers and escaping captivity. She retrieved the [[Tesseract]] but gave it to her allies and told them to escape while she remained to fight the Starforce and buy time. Thinking she has the Tesseract, Yon-Rogg and the other Starforce members surrounded Vers to get it back. [[File:Yon-Rogg Gauntlets Laboratory.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg fights [[Vers]] with his [[Magnitron Gauntlets|gauntlets]]]] Yon-Rogg's tean charged at her, but she destroyed a metal bridge, causing a pile of debris to fall on Yon-Rogg. Later in the battle Yon-Rogg used his gauntlets to come out of the debris and fight her, throwing debris at her and eventually getting her stuck at a wall under a pile of rubble. However, Danvers used her overwhelmingly powerful cosmic powers to easily break free, revealing that she didn't have the Tesseract. Learning their plans for diversion and unable to defeat her, Yon-Rogg fled the battle scene to retrieve the Tesseract. Yon-Rogg arrived at the lab's hanger just as [[Nick Fury]], [[Goose]] and the [[Skrulls]] were taking off in a [[Quadjet]] piloted by [[Maria Rambeau]]. He and other Kree soldiers tried to shoot it down with their pistols. He shot [[Talos]] in the shoulder, but he survived and the Quadjet escaped, destroying the ''[[Helion]]'' in the process. Yon-Rogg then ordered [[Minn-Erva]] to take one of the lab's [[Kree Drop Ships|space pods]] to follow them, but as he tried to take a pod to follow them himself, Vers got to him and tried to damage the pod. He used his gauntlets to knock her off the pod mid-air, and went after the Quadjet and shot at it, causing minor damage to it. But unbeknownst to him Vers was now able to fly, and she followed and damaged his pod, causing him to crash-land in the [[Mojave Desert]].{{Ref|CM}} ====Final Showdown==== [[File:CapMarvl28.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg and [[Carol Danvers]] face off]] {{Quote|I can't go back empty-handed."
"You won't be empty-handed. I'm sending you with a message. Tell the [[Supreme Intelligence]] that I'm coming to end it. The [[Kree-Skrull War|war]], the lies, all of it."
"You can't do this.|Yon-Rogg and [[Captain Marvel]]|Captain Marvel (film)}} With his [[Kree Drop Ships|space pod]] half destroyed by [[Captain Marvel]], Yon-Rogg prepared for a final showdown against his former teammate in the [[Mojave Desert]]. Exiting from his spacecraft, Yon-Rogg took his weapons ready to fight, but then put them away, and firmly stated that he was proud of Danvers for having improved her powers. [[File:Yon-RoggConfrontedByVers-InSand.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg challenges [[Carol Danvers]]]] Yon-Rogg arrogantly encouraged Danvers to prove her worth by facing him without her powers. As he was preparing a stance for a fair fight, Danvers shrugged him off and struck him with a photon blast, propelling him away towards a rocky wall, incapacitating him. Utterly defeated, Yon-Rogg was spared by Danvers, who resolved to send him back to [[Hala]], claiming that she did not need to prove anything to him. [[File:Yon-RoggInsideASpaceship-EndScene.png|{{AltFloat}}|Yon-Rogg is defeated by [[Carol Danvers]]]] As he was strapped into his spacecraft, Yon-Rogg told her that he couldn't go back with his hands empty, to which Danvers affirmed him that he would be sent back with a message to the [[Supreme Intelligence]]: that she would be going after the [[Kree Empire]] to finish the [[Kree-Skrull War]]. Yon-Rogg responded that she couldn't do it before she used a smaller photon blast to kick start the spacecraft's engine, sending him back from [[Earth]] to Hala.{{Ref|CM}}{{Clear}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== *'''Kree Physiology''': As a [[Kree]], Yon-Rogg possesses the typical physical abilities of his species, such as superhuman strength, durability, agility, stamina, reflexes and an efficient healing factor. He used these attributes to become one of the [[Kree Empire]]'s best warriors and also the leader of the [[Starforce]]. **'''Superhuman Strength''': Like all Kree, Yon-Rogg is very strong and possesses greater physical strength. Yog-Rogg was able to defeat [[Vers]] during all his training, with his punches being strong enough to knock her down and cause a small nosebleed. During the battle at the [[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]], after incapacitating her with his control over gravity thanks to his [[Magnitron Gauntlets|gauntlets]], Yon-Rogg hit Captain Marvel with enough force to send her flying away. **'''Superhuman Durability''': [[File:Yon-Rogg (Earth 1989).png|thumb|250px|Yon-Rogg withstands a [[Tesseract]] explosion]] Yon-Rogg's body is very resistant to physical damage, allowing him to resist great forces of impacts, as well as the photon blasts of [[Captain Marvel]]. Yon-Rogg also survived several metal beams that fell on him and the forced landing of his [[Kree Drop Ships|ship]]. **'''Superhuman Agility''': Yon-Rogg naturally has great agility which he uses in battle. Yon-Rogg was even able to match and even outmaneuver [[Vers]] when she had not unlocked her full powers with agile combat moves. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Yon-Rogg could fight for great periods of time in battle which helped him capture many [[Skrulls]] throughout his years of fighting. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Yon-Rogg possessed tremendous reflexes allowing him to dodge many blows from [[Vers]] and the [[Skrulls]]. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': As with all Kree, Yon-Rogg possesses a healing factor which allows him to recover from injuries in a short amount of time. **'''Longevity''': Yon-Rogg possesses a long lifespan. This trait is common among [[Kree]]. *'''[[Magnitron Gauntlets]] Capabilities''': As a part of Yon-Rogg's [[Starforce Uniform]], he had [[Magnitron Gauntlets]] attached to his wrists that he used to manipulate gravities in ways to gain the upper hand on his opponents. **'''Gravity Manipulation''': [[File:CapMarvl10.jpg|thumb|250px|Yon-Rogg attacks [[Skrulls]] with his [[Magnitron Gauntlets|gauntlets]]]] Once he activates the [[Magnitron Gauntlets]] incorporated into his suit, Yon-Rogg is able to utilize his surroundings for offensive or defensive capabilities. During the mission to [[Torfa]], Yon-Rogg used the gauntlets to send large groups of [[Skrulls]] flying through the air. Later, he used the gauntlets to catch [[Carol Danvers]] and knock her out. Yon-Rogg was also able to throw several metallic objects at Danvers, even managing to leave her trapped against the wall when manipulating the metals, although Danvers could finally free herself by using her powers. ===Abilities=== *'''Master Combatant''': [[File:Yon-Rogg & Carol Danvers.png|thumb|250px|Yon-Rogg sparring with [[Vers]]]] As the leader of the [[Starforce]], Yon-Rogg is highly trained in both armed and unarmed combat. Yon-Rogg was able to overcome [[Vers]], during his workouts, easily. Yon-Rogg also defeated several [[Skrulls]] during the mission to [[Torfa]] and was able to stay in combat against Captain Marvel, for a short time, in the battle that took place at the [[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]]. He used his knowledge in combat to train Vers to become one of the [[Kree Empire]]'s fiercest warriors. *'''Expert Marksman''': Yon-Rogg is accomplished in the use of Kree firearms. Yon-Rogg was able to use a gun to kill [[Mar-Vell]] and later during the battle against the [[Skrulls]] in Torfa. Yon-Rogg was able to accurately shoot and kill [[Norex]] with his [[Kree Pistol]]. *'''Expert Tactician''': As leader of the [[Starforce]], Yon-Rogg formulated plans to capture the [[Skrulls]] and/or kill them. However, he sought advice from the [[Supreme Intelligence]] many times to help him with his strategy. *'''Expert Pilot''': Yon-Rogg is a highly trained pilot. Yon-Rogg was able to stay in aerial combat against Carol Danvers and also able to pilot a Kree escape pod with relative ease. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''[[Magnitron Gauntlets]]''': Yon-Rogg uses two special gravity-control gauntlets designed by [[Kree]] to fight his opponents. *'''[[Kree Pistol]]''': Yon-Rogg used a Kree gun to kill [[Mar-Vell]] and multiple [[Skrulls]]. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Starforce Uniform]]''': [[File:Yon-Rogg (Captain Marvel).png|thumb|250px|Yon-Rogg wearing his [[Starforce Uniform]]]] Yon-Rogg possesses a uniform of [[Kree]] origin to use on his [[Starforce]] missions. The uniform had a built-in universal translator that allows its wearer to understand the language of anyone they interact with and be able to speak their language back to them. The uniform also contains a device capable of analyzing and identifying any object placed within it on the uniform's sleeve. The uniform's color scheme can also be customized to suit the user's preferences via a device located on the uniform's sleeve, though Yon-Rogg himself never had any reason to do this and was disgusted upon discovering that [[Carol Danvers]] had changed her uniform's color. It also includes a collapsible helmet that can fit around the wearer's head. The helmet can generate its own atmosphere and allows the wearer to breathe in the vacuum of space, as well as underwater, where it will give off a blue glow to provide light. ===Vehicles=== *'''''[[Helion]]''''': Yon-Rogg used the ''Helion'' during his missions with the [[Starforce]]. He traveled to [[Torfa]] with the spaceship during an extraction mission, which turned into an [[Ambush in Torfa|ambush]] by the [[Skrulls]]. Yon-Rogg ordered his team to retreat to the ''Helion'' when the situation got out of control. *'''[[Kree Drop Ships|Kree Space Pod]]''': Yon-Rogg used a Kree Space Pod as a means to escape [[Captain Marvel]] during the [[Battle at Mar-Vell's Laboratory]]. However, he crashed on [[Earth]] after a brief confrontation with his former ally. Captain Marvel decided to send Yon-Rogg to [[Hala]] with a message for the [[Supreme Intelligence]]. She put him inside the space pod and powered its engine with her photon blasts. *'''[[Mar-Vell's Laboratory]]''': Yon-Rogg and the rest of Starforce arrived at the orbital laboratory and captured Vers, Skrulls and their allies. Vers broke free and battled him and other Starforce members, defeating them all. He then tried to stop her allies from escaping the lab but failed and they destroyed the ''Helion'' on their way out, forcing him to take a space pod to follow them. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Father *Brother ===Allies=== *[[Kree Empire]] **[[Supreme Intelligence]] † - Superior **[[Starforce]] - Teammates ***[[Minn-Erva]] † ***[[Korath]] † - Former Teammate ***[[Att-Lass]] ***[[Bron-Char]] **[[Accusers]] ***[[Ronan the Accuser]] † **[[Sun-Val]] † **[[Soh-Larr]] *[[Rhomann Dey]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Skrulls]] **[[Talos]] † - Attempted Victim **[[Norex]] † - Victim **[[Soren]] † **[[G'iah]] *[[Nova Empire]] **[[Nova Corps]] *[[Mar-Vell]] † - Victim *[[Captain Marvel|Carol Danvers/Captain Marvel]] - Former Trainee, Former Teammate and Attempted Victim *[[Nick Fury]] *[[Maria Rambeau]] † *[[Goose]] {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== In chronological order: *{{Appearance|CMSotR}} **{{Appearance|CMSML}} (concurrent events) *{{Appearance|CM}} **{{Appearance|TM}} (flashbacks) **{{Appearance|CMA}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Yon-Rogg is a Colonel of the [[w:c:marvel:Imperial Kree Army (Earth-616)|Imperial Kree Army]] who kidnapped [[Carol Danvers]] but was defeated by [[Mar-Vell]], resulting in the explosion of the [[w:c:marvel:Psyche-Magnitron|Psyche-Magnitron]]. The explosion transformed Danvers to a Kree-Human hybrid and gave Yon-Rogg similar powers, as he started to call himself '''Magnitron'''. *[[Jude Law]] was originally listed as [[Mar-Vell]]. *In a [[List of Deleted Scenes|deleted scene]] in ''[[Captain Marvel (film)|Captain Marvel]]'', it was revealed that Yon-Rogg sees himself when he meets the [[Supreme Intelligence]]. This implies that Yon-Rogg considers himself the person he most admires. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Jude Law]] described Yon-Rogg as being "''driven by a belief in the divine leadership of the [[Kree]] people, so he's almost a devout warrior — unquestioning, conservative, but inspirational.''"[https://ew.com/movies/2018/09/06/jude-law-teases-character-captain-marvel/ Jude Law teases his 'devout warrior' character in ''Captain Marvel''] *Originally, Yon-Rogg's final fight against [[Vers]] was going to harken back their initial training fight on the beginning of the film, but directors [[Anna Boden]] and [[Ryan Fleck]] decided to rewrite it as they felt that Vers doesn't care about following Yon-Rogg's own rules anymore.[https://comicbook.com/marvel/2019/06/13/captain-marvel-directors-original-yon-rogg-carol-fight-changed/ Captain Marvel Directors Reveal Original Yon-Rogg Carol Fight And Why They Changed It] *{{WPS|Keanu Reeves}} turned down the role of Yon-Rogg before [[Jude Law]] was cast, due to scheduling conflicts with ''{{WPS|John Wick: Chapter 3 – Parabellum}}''.[https://comicbook.com/marvel/2019/02/03/captain-marvel-jude-laws-role-was-almost-played-by-keanu-reeves/ 'Captain Marvel': Jude Law's Role Was Almost Played By Keanu Reeves] *One of the preliminary ideas for the [[List of Post-credits Scenes|post-credits scene]] of ''[[Captain Marvel (film)|Captain Marvel]]'' had Yon-Rogg crashing on [[Sakaar]] after leaving [[Earth]], similar to how [[Thor]] crashed on Sakaar in ''[[Thor: Ragnarok]]''.[https://www.inverse.com/article/53973-captain-marvel-thor-ragnarok-crossover Captain Marvel's Post-Credits Scene Was Almost a 'Thor: Ragnarok' Crossover] *[[Colin Follenweider]] was a stunt double for [[Jude Law]] in the role of Yon-Rogg. *[[James Henderson]] was a stand-in for [[Jude Law]] in the role of Yon-Rogg. ==References==
